summary.intent: Audit all 69 bundled skills for Hermes-specific dependencies, add Moltis-native `requires:` frontmatter blocks, and remove all Hermes references from skill bodies.
summary.outcome: 28 skills received `requires:` frontmatter with binary names and install methods; 66 files were cleaned of Hermes references; the webhook-subscriptions skill was fully rewritten for the Moltis RPC API. 5 remaining 'Hermes' references are legitimate (Nous Hermes model name) and intentionally kept.

repo: Moltis skill frontmatter supports `requires:` with `bins` (required binaries), `any_bins` (at least one needed), and `install` (install instructions) — defined in crates/skills/src/types.rs
Skills originated from hermes-agent@9f22977f carry `origin:` metadata and `metadata.hermes:` tags that are Hermes-specific and should be stripped for Moltis
Moltis-native frontmatter fields are: name, description, origin, requires, platforms, license — all others (metadata, dependencies, version, author) are Hermes artifacts
6 skills have `platforms:` restrictions (macOS/Linux specific); 1 skill has a non-MIT license
The webhook system uses RPC API (`webhooks.*`) with built-in source profiles (GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, Linear, PagerDuty, Sentry) and supports per_delivery/per_entity/named_session modes

code.finding: Defines the `requires:` frontmatter schema: `bins` (all required), `any_bins` (one of), and `install` (how to install)
Fully rewritten from Hermes CLI (`hermes webhook`) to Moltis RPC API (`webhooks.*`) with auth modes, session modes, filtering, and troubleshooting

workflow: Initial agent-based audit was too shallow; a direct bash grep across all files was more reliable for bulk dependency discovery
Binary names in `requires:` must be verified — grep output truncation caused one wrong binary name (`a` instead of `memo` for apple-notes) that had to be fixed
Bulk sed-based cleanup of frontmatter fields across 66 files is practical when fields follow consistent YAML patterns; individual file edits needed for edge cases
Skills that use Python in-body scripts (e.g. uv run) still list `python3` as a required bin even though uv manages the environment

friction: First audit agent pass was too shallow and missed most skills, requiring a manual re-scan with bash grep
grep output truncation caused an incorrect binary name (`a`) to be injected for apple-notes, requiring a follow-up fix pass
Bulk regex cleanup cannot handle all Hermes references in code blocks or prose context — 9 files needed individual attention after the bulk pass
open_items: github/* skills still contain `~/.hermes/` paths that should be replaced with Moltis equivalents (deferred cosmetic cleanup)
Some skills still have 'Hermes Integration' section prose that references Hermes workflows — noted as cosmetic and deferred
41 skills have no `requires:` block — confirm these truly have no external binary dependencies beyond curl/git/python3
